_comment: Documented compat deltas. Each entry suppresses a matching DiffRecord from the active section of summary.md and surfaces it under '## Suppressed (known deltas)' with its reason + regressionTest. See docs/registry/api-compat-deltas.md.

deltas.reason: v3's updateCache is phase-aware: 200 + Git-refresh duration while git-sourced components remain (migration-status git>0), 410 Gone once fully migrated to the DB (git==0, with the Git resolver still parsing components). A db-mode compat candidate is fully migrated (auto-migrate -> git==0) so it returns 410 here; the git-mode candidate (no migration, git>0) returns 200 and matches baseline, so this delta does NOT appear in known-deltas-git.json (empty). baselineValue intentionally omitted: under parallel test load the V1 baseline can transport-fail this heavy operation (Git DSL re-clone) before the HTTP client's timeout — same root cause regardless of whether baseline answers 200 or transport-fails (0). id17 #3666 reproduced the transport-failure variant. Confirmed intentional 2026-05-15; updated for phase-aware 2026-06-01.
deltas.regressionTest: ComponentsRegistryServiceControllerUpdateCacheTest.git equal to zero retires the endpoint with 410 and does not re-read

deltas.reason: Symptom of the STATUS_CODE_DIFF entry above — when the V1 baseline transport-fails the heavy /updateCache call under parallel load, no response headers come back and only candidate (which answers 410 with a JSON ErrorResponse body) reports a content-type. Suppressed together with the status-code deprecation delta.
deltas.regressionTest: ComponentsRegistryServiceControllerUpdateCacheTest.git equal to zero retires the endpoint with 410 and does not re-read

deltas.reason: #256 stale-git bleed-through fix. For a DB-sourced project whose requested version is absent from the DB, the V1/git baseline can still serve a STALE VCSSettings as 200 from legacy DSL, while the v3 candidate routes by resolved component source (ComponentRoutingResolver.resolverForProject) and correctly returns 404. This delta only materialises when the smoke/trace set happens to probe a db-sourced project at a version git still stale-matches; baselineValue omitted because the baseline value is the stale 200 we are intentionally diverging from. The authoritative regression guard is the unit test (this entry is defensive documentation, same convention as the updateCache deltas above).

#256 stale-git bleed-through fix (Distribution counterpart of the vcs-settings entry above). DB-sourced project + version absent from DB: git baseline may serve a stale Distribution as 200; v3 candidate routes by resolved component source and returns 404. Authoritative guard is the unit test; this entry is defensive documentation.
